useCases.title: Dormant Account Remediation Agent
statusQuo: Analysts export dormant-account lists from Temenos Transact into spreadsheets each month and reconcile them by hand against state escheatment calendars.
Customer re-contact letters are drafted one at a time, so thousands of accounts sit past the outreach deadline every cycle.
Missed escheatment deadlines trigger state penalties and remediation projects that pull staff off daily deposit operations.
agentification: Scans Temenos Transact and BigQuery nightly to classify accounts by dormancy stage and applicable state escheatment rules.
Generates personalized re-contact letters and creates ServiceNow tasks for accounts requiring branch follow-up.
Escalates accounts approaching statutory deadlines to the operations queue with a prepared escheatment filing package.

useCases.title: Account Opening Document Follow-Up Agent

kpis.label: Average days to cure a document exception
Branch staff hours chasing documents per week

statusQuo: Branch staff keep personal spreadsheets of missing signature cards and ID documents because Temenos Transact exceptions are only reviewed weekly.
Follow-up calls and emails to customers are ad hoc, so exceptions age for weeks before anyone notices.
Audit findings repeatedly cite unsigned account agreements that were never routed for e-signature.
agentification: Detects new-account document exceptions in Temenos Transact the moment an account is booked and matches them against required-document checklists.
Routes missing agreements to DocuSign and drafts customer reminder messages on a cadence until the packet is complete.
Notifies the branch operations manager with a daily aging summary and escalates exceptions older than ten days.

useCases.title: Overdraft Fee Dispute Triage Agent

kpis.label: Disputes handled per FTE per day

statusQuo: Every overdraft fee complaint requires an agent to pull transaction history from Temenos Transact and manually reconstruct posting order.
Refund decisions vary widely by branch because there is no consistent view of the customer's fee history and relationship value.
Dispute tickets pile up in ServiceNow with no prioritization, so high-value customers wait as long as everyone else.
agentification: Reconstructs the posting sequence and fee history from Temenos Transact and BigQuery for each incoming dispute ticket.
Recommends a refund or denial with a written rationale based on fee policy, prior waivers, and relationship depth.
Routes clear-cut refunds for one-click approval and escalates pattern-abuse cases to the service manager.

useCases.title: Deposit Attrition Early Warning Monitor
statusQuo: The deposits team learns about large balance run-off from month-end Looker dashboards, weeks after the money has already left.
Analysts manually stitch Temenos Transact balance trends with rate-sheet data to guess which customers are rate shopping.
Retention offers go out in blanket campaigns because nobody can rank individual accounts by flight risk.
agentification: Scores every deposit relationship weekly for attrition risk using balance velocity, external transfer patterns, and competitor rate spreads.
Generates a ranked retention worklist with a recommended offer and talking points for each at-risk household.
Publishes an attrition heatmap to Looker and notifies product managers when a segment's outflow breaches threshold.

useCases.title: Branch Cash Position Forecast Engine
statusQuo: Branch managers forecast vault and ATM cash needs on gut feel plus last week's numbers keyed into a spreadsheet.
Payroll Fridays and local events routinely cause cash-outs that force expensive emergency armored-carrier shipments.
Regional operations has no consolidated view of vault positions, so excess cash sits idle at some branches while others run short.
agentification: Forecasts daily cash demand per branch and ATM from Temenos Transact transaction history, seasonality, and local payroll calendars.
Generates optimized shipment and return orders for the armored-carrier schedule and publishes them to each branch every morning.
Escalates predicted cash-out risks to regional operations 48 hours in advance with a recommended transfer plan.

useCases.title: Loan Covenant Monitoring Agent

kpis.label: Days from financials received to covenant result

statusQuo: Analysts track hundreds of financial covenants in spreadsheets keyed off loan agreements stored as PDFs in nCino.
Borrower financial statements arrive by email and sit unprocessed for weeks before ratios are calculated by hand.
Breaches are often discovered months late, forcing hurried waiver negotiations and criticized-asset write-ups.
agentification: Extracts covenant terms from executed loan agreements in nCino and builds a per-facility testing calendar automatically.
Computes leverage, coverage, and liquidity ratios from incoming borrower financials and records pass/fail results in BigQuery.
Escalates breaches and near-breaches to the credit risk officer with a drafted waiver memo and trend analysis.

useCases.title: Commercial Credit Memo Drafting Agent

kpis.label: Deals underwritten per analyst per month

statusQuo: Analysts spend two to three days per deal copying borrower financials, collateral details, and exposure data from nCino into a Word memo template.
Industry and market context is researched from scratch for every deal, even for repeat borrowers in familiar sectors.
Credit committee sends memos back repeatedly for missing global-exposure tables and inconsistent ratio calculations.
agentification: Assembles borrower financial spreads, global exposure, and collateral positions from nCino and BigQuery into the bank's memo template.
Drafts the narrative sections including industry outlook, repayment analysis, and risk mitigants for analyst review.
Flags policy exceptions such as LTV or hold-limit overages and generates the required exception justification section.

useCases.title: Loan Documentation Exception Clearing Agent
statusQuo: The exception report from nCino runs to thousands of lines and is reviewed line by line in a monthly meeting.
Chasing missing UCC filings, insurance certificates, and signed amendments means swivel-chairing between nCino, email, and county filing sites.
Regulators cite the aged-exception backlog every exam cycle, and collateral perfection gaps surface only when a loan defaults.
agentification: Classifies every new nCino exception by risk severity and cure path the moment it is created.
Drafts borrower and insurer request letters, routes signature items through DocuSign, and opens ServiceNow tasks for internal cure steps.
Notifies the loan operations manager weekly with an aging waterfall and escalates exceptions on classified credits immediately.

useCases.title: Early Delinquency Outreach Orchestrator

kpis.label: Roll rate from 30 to 60 days past due
Accounts worked per collector per day

statusQuo: Collectors dial down a static delinquency list sorted by balance, ignoring which borrowers are likeliest to self-cure.
Hardship program options are explained inconsistently, and eligible borrowers are rarely offered them at first contact.
Notes live in three systems, so a borrower who already made a promise to pay gets called again the next day.
agentification: Segments early-stage delinquencies nightly by cure probability and recommends the contact channel and time most likely to reach each borrower.
Drafts compliant outreach messages with pre-qualified hardship options based on the borrower's loan terms and payment history.
Creates a prioritized daily worklist per collector and suppresses accounts with active promises or pending payments.

useCases.title: Credit Portfolio Concentration Monitor

kpis.label: Analyst hours on quarterly portfolio review

statusQuo: Concentration reporting by industry, geography, and sponsor is compiled quarterly in spreadsheets from nCino extracts and is stale on arrival.
New deals are approved without visibility into whether they push a sector or single-name limit past board thresholds.
CRE and leveraged-lending concentrations flagged by regulators require weeks of scramble to quantify and explain.
agentification: Recomputes exposure against every board concentration limit daily as commitments and outstandings change in nCino.
Simulates the concentration impact of pipeline deals and notifies underwriters when a pending approval would breach a limit.
Publishes a live concentration dashboard to Looker and drafts the quarterly board narrative with trend commentary.

useCases.title: Fraud Alert Triage Agent
statusQuo: Analysts work through thousands of NICE Actimize alerts a day, the vast majority of which close as false positives after fifteen minutes of research each.
Context gathering means opening five systems to check device history, travel patterns, and recent contact-center interactions per alert.
Genuine account-takeover alerts wait in the same FIFO queue as low-risk card alerts, so real fraud runs for hours before anyone looks.
agentification: Enriches each Actimize alert with device, transaction, and customer-history context from BigQuery and drafts a disposition summary.
Auto-closes alerts matching well-understood benign patterns with a documented rationale and full audit trail.
Escalates high-risk alerts to the front of the queue and routes confirmed-fraud cases to ServiceNow with the case file pre-built.

useCases.title: Wire Exception Repair Agent

kpis.label: Cutoff misses due to repair backlog

statusQuo: Wires kick out of the FIS Payments Hub for malformed beneficiary details and queue for manual repair by a small specialist team.
Repair clerks look up correct BIC codes and account formats across bank directories and prior payment history by hand.
End-of-day cutoffs are routinely missed during volume spikes, generating customer complaints and compensation claims.
agentification: Diagnoses each repair-queue wire in the FIS Payments Hub and proposes corrected beneficiary fields from payment history and bank directories.
Applies high-confidence repairs automatically and routes ambiguous cases to a clerk with the top candidate fixes ranked.
Notifies the operations manager when queue depth threatens a cutoff and escalates time-critical, high-value wires first.

useCases.title: Card Dispute Chargeback Orchestrator
statusQuo: Specialists manually classify each dispute into network reason codes and assemble evidence packets transaction by transaction.
Visa and Mastercard representment deadlines are tracked in a shared calendar, and misses mean automatic write-offs.
Provisional credit decisions are inconsistent because Reg E and Reg Z timelines are computed by hand for every case.
agentification: Classifies incoming disputes to the correct network reason code and computes Reg E/Reg Z provisional-credit deadlines automatically.
Generates the representment evidence packet from FIS Payments Hub transaction data, merchant records, and customer history.
Tracks every network deadline, escalates cases at risk of expiry, and creates ServiceNow follow-ups for merchant responses.

useCases.title: ACH Return Root Cause Analyzer

kpis.label: Time to identify a problem originator

statusQuo: Return-code analysis is a monthly spreadsheet exercise that lumps R01 insufficient-funds noise together with serious unauthorized-debit signals.
Corporate originators creeping toward Nacha return-rate thresholds are discovered only after the network sends a warning letter.
Nobody traces repeat returns back to bad account-validation practices at specific origination channels.
agentification: Analyzes daily ACH return files from the FIS Payments Hub, clustering returns by originator, SEC code, and root cause.
Notifies relationship owners when an originator trends toward the 0.5% unauthorized threshold with a projected breach date.
Publishes a return-rate scorecard to Looker and recommends validation-control fixes for the worst offending channels.

useCases.title: Fraud Rule Tuning Analyzer
statusQuo: Detection rules in NICE Actimize are reviewed quarterly at best, so decayed rules keep generating thousands of worthless alerts.
Measuring a rule's true hit rate requires analysts to join alert outcomes with confirmed-fraud data manually in spreadsheets.
New fraud patterns from the industry sharing groups take months to translate into deployed rule changes.
agentification: Evaluates every Actimize rule weekly against confirmed-fraud outcomes in BigQuery, scoring precision, recall, and dollar coverage.
Recommends threshold adjustments and retirement candidates with a projected impact on alert volume and detection rate.
Drafts the rule-change proposal document with backtest evidence for the fraud strategy governance meeting.

useCases.title: Beneficial Ownership Refresh Agent
statusQuo: Analysts manually re-collect ownership structures for thousands of entity customers on rolling refresh cycles tracked in Fenergo work queues.
Corporate registry lookups, document requests, and 25%-threshold calculations are repeated from scratch even when nothing has changed.
Refresh backlogs push high-risk entities past their due dates, a finding repeated in the last two regulatory exams.
agentification: Pre-screens each due refresh by comparing Fenergo records against corporate registries and flags entities with actual ownership changes.
Drafts targeted outreach requesting only the changed documents and routes certification forms through DocuSign.
Recalculates beneficial-ownership percentages, updates the Fenergo profile for analyst approval, and escalates newly identified high-risk owners.

useCases.title: AML Alert Investigation Agent

kpis.label: QA rework rate on case narratives

statusQuo: Investigators spend most of each case gathering transaction history, KYC data, and negative news across six systems before analysis even begins.
Case narratives are written free-form, so quality varies by investigator and QA sends a fifth of them back for rewrite.
A growing alert backlog forces risk-ranked queue skipping that the BSA officer must defend to examiners.
agentification: Assembles a complete investigation file for each Actimize alert, including transaction flows, counterparty graphs, and screening history from BigQuery.
Drafts a structured case narrative summarizing activity patterns against the customer's expected profile.
Recommends a close or escalate disposition with cited evidence and routes escalations to the SAR team with the file attached.

useCases.title: SAR Filing Preparation Agent
statusQuo: SAR narratives are drafted by hand from investigation notes, and the 30-day filing clock is tracked in a spreadsheet.
Writers re-key subject, account, and transaction details into the FinCEN form, introducing transcription errors that trigger corrections.
Continuing-activity reviews at 90 days are frequently missed because there is no systematic tickler process.
agentification: Drafts the SAR narrative in FinCEN's expected structure directly from the escalated Actimize case file and transaction data.
Pre-populates form fields from system-of-record data and validates them against FinCEN error rules before filing.
Tracks 30-day deadlines and 90-day continuing-activity reviews, escalating anything at risk to the AML compliance officer.

useCases.title: Periodic KYC Review Orchestrator

kpis.label: Reviews completed per analyst per week

statusQuo: Periodic review scheduling is a manual allocation exercise, and analysts cherry-pick easy retail cases while complex entities age.
Each review starts with an hour of data gathering to see whether the customer's activity still matches the documented profile.
Risk-rating changes discovered during review take weeks to propagate into monitoring thresholds and relationship approvals.
agentification: Orchestrates the review pipeline by risk tier and due date, pre-building each case file with activity-versus-profile comparisons from BigQuery.
Auto-completes low-risk reviews with no material changes and generates a documented rationale for QA sampling.
Routes profile mismatches and proposed risk-rating upgrades to senior analysts and creates ServiceNow items for downstream approvals.

useCases.title: Sanctions Screening Hit Analyzer
statusQuo: Over 98% of sanctions screening hits are name-match false positives, each dispositioned manually with a copy-pasted rationale.
Analysts compare dates of birth, addresses, and identifiers across Fenergo records and list entries by eye under intense time pressure.
Payment-screening queues back up during OFAC list updates, delaying legitimate wires for hours and drawing customer complaints.
agentification: Compares each hit's identifiers against Fenergo customer data and list-entry details, scoring true-match likelihood with cited evidence.
Auto-clears obvious mismatches with a standardized, auditable rationale and releases the associated payments.
Escalates potential true matches to a senior analyst within minutes with a side-by-side identity comparison prepared.

useCases.title: Portfolio Review Preparation Agent

kpis.label: Client-facing hours per advisor per week

statusQuo: Advisors spend hours before each review pulling performance, allocation drift, and held-away asset data into a slide deck by hand.
Talking points about life events and service issues are reconstructed from memory and scattered Salesforce notes.
Review meetings slip past their annual due dates for a third of the book, a growing supervisory concern.
agentification: Generates a complete review packet with performance versus benchmark, allocation drift, fee summary, and cash-flow analysis for each upcoming meeting.
Drafts a personalized agenda from Salesforce interaction history, flagged life events, and open service items.
Notifies the advisor of reviews coming due and recommends rebalancing or tax-loss harvesting topics worth raising.

useCases.title: Suitability Drift Review Monitor

kpis.label: Time to detect a suitability mismatch

statusQuo: Suitability surveillance is a quarterly manual sample, so most accounts are never checked between onboarding and a complaint.
Risk-tolerance profiles in Salesforce go stale for years while portfolio risk drifts with markets and trading activity.
Compliance discovers concentration and risk mismatches only when FINRA inquiries or customer complaints arrive.
agentification: Scores every account monthly for drift between documented risk tolerance, investment objectives, and actual portfolio composition.
Creates ServiceNow review cases for material mismatches with the evidence and the applicable suitability rule cited.
Drafts client re-profiling outreach for advisors when tolerance documentation is stale or contradicted by stated objectives.

useCases.title: Advisor Next Best Action Engine
statusQuo: Relationship managers learn about maturing CDs, large cash inflows, and executive stock vesting events weeks late or not at all.
Prioritizing the day's outreach means scrolling Salesforce lists with no signal about which client actually needs a call.
Money-in-motion moments pass to competitors because the first conversation happens after the funds have moved.
agentification: Detects money-in-motion signals such as large deposits, maturing instruments, and rollover-eligible balances from BigQuery in near real time.
Recommends the next best action per client with a suggested product conversation and compliant talking points.
Creates prioritized Salesforce tasks and notifies the relationship manager when a top-decile client shows attrition signals.

useCases.title: Wealth Client Onboarding Orchestrator

kpis.label: Days from signed agreement to funded account
Onboarding status inquiries to service desk

statusQuo: New-client paperwork bounces between the advisor, operations, and custodian with a third of packets rejected as not in good order.
ACAT transfer status is checked manually at the custodian portal, and clients call repeatedly asking where their money is.
Each onboarding step is tracked in a different tool, so nobody owns the end-to-end timeline.
agentification: Validates account applications against custodian requirements before submission and routes corrected forms through DocuSign.
Tracks every onboarding milestone including ACAT transfers, publishing live status to Salesforce for the advisor and client portal.
Escalates stalled transfers to operations with the custodian rejection reason and drafted follow-up already prepared.

useCases.title: Advisory Fee Billing Anomaly Analyzer

kpis.label: Fee errors caught before client billing

statusQuo: Quarterly fee runs are spot-checked on a small sample, so breakpoint misses and wrong household groupings reach client statements.
Fee schedule exceptions negotiated by advisors live in emails and are applied inconsistently at billing time.
Every SEC exam requests fee-billing accuracy evidence that takes the operations team weeks to assemble.
agentification: Recomputes every account's expected fee from the contracted schedule, householding rules, and billable-asset data in BigQuery.
Flags discrepancies against the actual fee run with a root-cause classification before invoices are released.
Generates the quarterly billing-accuracy attestation package and publishes exception trends to Looker for compliance.

useCases.title: Intraday Liquidity Forecasting Engine
statusQuo: The morning liquidity forecast is assembled in spreadsheets from Murex positions, nostro statements, and payment schedules before markets open.
Large unexpected outflows force same-day borrowing at penalty rates because the forecast missed a settlement spike.
The bank holds an oversized liquidity buffer as insurance against forecast error, dragging on net interest income.
agentification: Forecasts intraday cash positions per currency by combining Murex MX.3 settlement ladders, historical payment flows, and scheduled client activity.
Publishes an hourly-updated funding plan to Looker with recommended money-market actions per currency.
Escalates projected shortfalls to the treasury manager hours before cutoffs with ranked funding options and costs.

useCases.title: VaR Limit Breach Triage Monitor

kpis.label: Time from breach to explained root cause
Limit excesses aged past policy deadline

statusQuo: When a desk breaches a VaR or sensitivity limit, analysts manually decompose the risk run to find which trades and factors drove it.
Breach memos, desk-head acknowledgments, and remediation tracking are handled over email with no consistent audit trail.
Stale limit excesses linger past the policy escalation deadline, drawing internal-audit findings every year.
agentification: Decomposes each Murex MX.3 limit breach to the contributing trades, risk factors, and market moves within minutes of the risk run.
Drafts the breach notification memo with root-cause analysis and routes acknowledgment tasks through ServiceNow.
Escalates unresolved excesses automatically up the delegation chain when policy deadlines approach.

useCases.title: End-of-Day P&L Attribution Analyzer

kpis.label: Time to investigate a P&L break

statusQuo: Product controllers manually reconcile flash P&L against the Murex end-of-day run, chasing breaks across trades, market data, and new deals.
Explaining daily P&L into risk-based buckets is a spreadsheet exercise that regularly slips past the sign-off deadline.
Recurring breaks from the same booking-model issues are rediscovered from scratch every time they appear.
agentification: Runs risk-based P&L attribution per desk each night, decomposing moves into market factors, new trades, and amendments from Murex MX.3 data.
Matches residual breaks against a learned library of historical break patterns and drafts the likely explanation for controller review.
Publishes the attribution pack to Looker by 7am and escalates desks with unexplained P&L above threshold.

useCases.title: Counterparty Credit Exposure Monitor

kpis.label: Time to full counterparty exposure view
Wrong-way risk cases identified per quarter

statusQuo: A consolidated view of derivative exposure, collateral, and ratings per counterparty is only available the next day after batch aggregation.
Margin-call disputes are worked from email threads, and aged disputes inflate uncollateralized exposure unnoticed.
Deteriorating counterparties are caught by quarterly reviews rather than market signals like CDS spread widening.
agentification: Aggregates potential future exposure, collateral balances, and netting-set details from Murex MX.3 into an intraday counterparty view.
Monitors market signals such as ratings actions and CDS moves, escalating counterparties whose risk profile deteriorates.
Drafts margin-call dispute summaries with valuation comparisons and recommends resolution actions to the collateral team.

useCases.title: Trade Confirmation Break Resolution Agent
statusQuo: Ops analysts compare inbound counterparty confirmations against Murex trade bookings field by field to find the discrepancy behind each break.
Chasing counterparties on unmatched trades is a manual email process with no systematic aging or prioritization.
Aged unconfirmed derivatives accumulate settlement and legal risk that surfaces painfully during audits.
agentification: Compares each unmatched confirmation against the Murex MX.3 booking and pinpoints the exact mismatched economic fields.
Drafts counterparty chaser messages and routes internal booking-error corrections to the desk via ServiceNow.
Escalates breaks by notional and age against ISDA timeliness targets, notifying the operations manager of aging risk daily.
